claims.claim: Tailwind CSS v4 is GA (current release v4.3, May 2026) and uses CSS-first configuration via @import "tailwindcss"; v4 dropped automatic tailwind.config.js loading and needs a build plugin (@tailwindcss/vite or @tailwindcss/postcss).
claims.raw_snippet: Tailwind CSS works by scanning all of your HTML files, JavaScript components, and any other templates for class names, generating the corresponding styles and then writing them to a static CSS file.

claims.claim: As of Tailwind v4.3 (May 2026) the logical inset utilities start-*/end-* are deprecated in favor of inset-s-*/inset-e-* (old names still work); margin/padding ms-/me-/ps-/pe- are unaffected.
claims.raw_snippet: The existing start-* and end-* utilities are still available, but they're now deprecated in favor of inset-s-* and inset-e-* so the whole API lines up with inset-bs-* and inset-be-*.

claims.claim: Tailwind provides built-in rtl: and ltr: variants for direction-specific styling, resolved via the CSS :dir() pseudo-class so they respond to dir="auto" as well as explicit dir="rtl".
claims.raw_snippet: Use the rtl and ltr variants to conditionally add styles in right-to-left and left-to-right modes respectively when building multi-directional layouts.

claims.claim: Tailwind v4 @theme inline resolves CSS variables at the use site, which is required when wiring next/font (which exposes the font as a runtime CSS variable); plain @theme cannot reference a runtime variable.
claims.raw_snippet: Use the inline option when you want your theme variables to be resolved inline, referencing other variables by value rather than by reference.

claims.claim: In Tailwind v4 the darkMode config key is gone; class-based dark mode is opted into in CSS with @custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));.
claims.raw_snippet: To use class-based dark mode instead of the prefers-color-scheme media query, use the @custom-variant directive to override the dark variant.

claims.claim: Heebo is a Hebrew UI font available on Google Fonts.
claims.raw_snippet: Heebo is a Hebrew and Latin typeface family available on Google Fonts.

claims.claim: Assistant is a Hebrew body font available on Google Fonts.
claims.raw_snippet: Assistant is a Hebrew and Latin sans-serif typeface available on Google Fonts.

claims.claim: fonts.gstatic.com is the Google Fonts static host serving font binary files; preconnect to it for faster loading.
claims.raw_snippet: fonts.gstatic.com is the Google Fonts static content host that serves the font binary files.

claims.claim: fonts.googleapis.com serves the Google Fonts stylesheet; preconnect to it for faster loading.
claims.raw_snippet: fonts.googleapis.com is the Google Fonts API host that serves the generated CSS stylesheet.

claims.claim: The Google Fonts css2 endpoint loads Heebo and Assistant with font-display: swap via a stylesheet link.
claims.raw_snippet: The css2 endpoint returns @font-face rules for the requested families (Heebo, Assistant) with the requested weights and display=swap.

claims.claim: The font-display: swap descriptor renders fallback text immediately and swaps in the web font when loaded, mitigating FOIT.
claims.raw_snippet: swap: Gives the font face a zero second block period and an infinite swap period.
